name: MSSM + SO(10) GUT + type-I seesaw + thermal leptogenesis (KKLT stabilisation)
summary: Classification: KNOWN PROGRAM. The canonical SUSY-GUT-string framework pursued from the 1980s through ~2010, before LHC null results eroded weak-scale SUSY. SO(10) unifies one SM family + N_R in the spinor 16, automatically giving the type-I seesaw with M_R near the GUT scale. Thermal leptogenesis via decay of the lightest N_R produces the baryon asymmetry through the sphaleron-converted L -> B route. MSSM provides gauge-coupling unification at M_GUT ~ 2x10^16 GeV and a neutralino LSP (if R-parity is conserved). KKLT-style moduli stabilisation embeds this in a string-landscape vacuum so the cosmological constant is selected anthropically. Listed here as the high-water mark of the 'unified BSM' program, with the explicit acknowledgement that LHC has pushed superpartners above ~1-2 TeV and the proton-decay bound on minimal SU(5) is in tension.

lineage.citation: Mohapatra and Senjanovic, 'Neutrino Mass and Spontaneous Parity Nonconservation', PRL 44, 912 (1980)
Fukugita and Yanagida, 'Baryogenesis without grand unification', PLB 174, 45 (1986)
Buchmuller, Di Bari, Plumacher, 'Leptogenesis for pedestrians', Annals Phys. 315, 305 (2005)
Kachru, Kallosh, Linde, Trivedi, 'de Sitter vacua in string theory', PRD 68, 046005 (2003)
Raby, 'Grand Unified Theories', in Particle Data Group review (2024)
